description: The 2020 collapse of the German payment processor Wirecard, where auditors discovered that €1.9 billion in cash on the balance sheet did not exist. Public opinion shifted from defending the tech giant to criticizing German financial regulators.

key_events.description: FT publishes reports questioning Wirecard's revenue model.
EY refuses to sign off on the 2019 financial statements.
Wirecard files for insolvency; CEO Markus Braun is arrested.
The German regulator BaFin admits to massive failures in their audit process.

calibration_notes: Models a scenario where the 'against' group (those critical of management/regulators) grows slowly as skeptical journalists are initially dismissed by the public.
